title: Laser diode reticule illumination
abstract: Provided is an illuminated reticle assembly (10) for an optical aiming device. The assembly (10) includes a reticle plate (12) has a first surface (14) on which a physical reticle pattern (16) is applied. A laser light source (38) is configured to project a beam of laser light into the reticle plate (16) at a first angle and toward an inner side of a second surface (18) at an angle of incidence that directs a reflected beam of laser light toward at least a selected portion of the physical reticle pattern (16). The reflected laser light illuminates the at least selected portion of the physical reticle pattern (16).
claims: Claims 1 . An illuminated reticle assembly for an optical aiming device, comprising: a reticle plate having a first surface on which a physical reticle pattern is applied; a laser light source configured to project a beam of laser light into the reticle plate at a first angle and toward an inner side of a second surface at an angle of incidence that directs a reflected beam of laser light toward at least a selected portion of the physical reticle pattern; the reflected laser light illuminating the at least selected portion of the physical reticle pattern. 2. The reticle assembly of claim 1 , wherein the reticle plate includes a peripheral surface formed at an oblique angle to the first surface and substantially orthogonal to the beam of laser light projected into the reticle plate. 3. The reticle assembly of claim 1 , further comprising a lens configured to focus the beam of laser light before it is projected into the reticle plate. 4. The reticle assembly of claim 1 , further comprising an attenuator to limit intensity of the projected beam of laser light to an eye-safe level. 5. The reticle assembly of claim 1 , further comprising a second, non-laser light source configured to illuminate at least a different portion of the physical reticle pattern. 6. The reticle assembly of claim 5, wherein the second light source is of a color contrasting with that of the laser light source. 7. The reticle assembly of claim 1 , wherein the physical reticle pattern is etched on the first surface. 8. An illuminated reticle assembly for an optical aiming device, comprising: a reticle plate having a first surface on which a physical reticle pattern is applied; a cover plate secured to the first surface of the reticle plate over the physical reticle pattern; a laser light source configured to project a beam of laser light into the reticle plate at a first angle and toward an inner-side surface of the cover plate at an angle of incidence that directs a reflected beam of laser light toward at least a selected portion of the physical reticle pattern; the laser light illuminating the at least selected portion of the physical reticle pattern. 9. The reticle assembly of claim 8, wherein the reticle plate includes a peripheral surface formed at an oblique angle to the inner-side surface and substantially orthogonal to the beam of laser light projected into the reticle plate. 10. The reticle assembly of claim 8, further comprising a lens configured to focus the beam of laser light before it is projected into the reticle plate. 1 1 . The reticle assembly of claim 8, further comprising an attenuator to limit intensity of the projected beam of laser light to an eye-safe level. 12. The reticle assembly of claim 8, further comprising a second, non-laser light source configured to illuminate at least a different portion of the physical reticle pattern. 13. The reticle assembly of claim 12, wherein the second light source is of a color contrasting with that of the laser light source. 14. The reticle assembly of claim 8, wherein the physical reticle pattern is etched on the first surface.
